results.evidence: api-routes.ts:925-927 calls judgeAssertion on both skillOutput and baselineOutput via Promise.all
baselinePassRate computation uses same formula as skillPassRate — tested in comparison-sse-events.test.ts
api-routes.ts:938 emits baseline_assertion_result SSE event, verified in comparison-sse-events.test.ts
api-routes.ts:1088 delta = passRate - baselinePassRate (not rubricAvg diff)
RunPanel.tsx:232 renders (delta * 100).toFixed(1)% — with pass rate delta this produces correct percentage
When both pass rates are 1.0, delta = 1.0 - 1.0 = 0.0, UI shows 0.0%
verdict.ts:11 — baselinePassRate: number = 0 added as 4th parameter
verdict.test.ts:90-99 — no assertionPassRate >= 0.8 returns INEFFECTIVE, tested with multiple combinations
api-routes.ts:1079 overall_pass_rate = passRate (skill pass rate, field name contextually clear)
api-routes.ts:1083-1085 skillPassRate and baselinePassRate are separate fields in comparison object
skill-create-routes.ts:471-483 adds 'Assertion Quality: Functional Over Formatting (CRITICAL)' section
prompt-builder.ts:99-103 adds best practice #8 on unit-test granularity
Both prompts warn against blank lines, paragraph count, whitespace, heading levels, bullet formatting
